title: Light Crispy Japanese Tuna Poke
description: A refreshing and light Japanese dish made with sashimi-grade tuna, black olives, and air-fried to perfection. Perfect for a date-night dinner.
instructions: Cut the sashimi-grade tuna into small cubes and place in a bowl.
In a small bowl, whisk together sushi rice vinegar, soy sauce, and honey.
Pour the dressing over the tuna and toss to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Add the black olives to the air fryer basket and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until crispy.
Remove the black olives from the air fryer and set aside.
Add the green onion to the air fryer basket and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until slightly caramelized.
Remove the green onion from the air fryer and set aside.
Add the sesame seeds to the air fryer basket and cook for 1 minute, or until lightly toasted.
Remove the sesame seeds from the air fryer and set aside.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the tuna from the dressing and place on a serving plate.
Drizzle the remaining dressing over the tuna and top with crispy black olives, green onion, and toasted sesame seeds.
